top_signals.title: U.S. Oil Shocks Don't Hit Like They Used To, Fed Study Finds
top_signals.summary: The United States still feels oil shocks. It just doesn't feel them the way it did when America was dancing to disco and waiting in gas lines. If the Fed is right, the idea that every oil shock leads to recession is outdated. A new study from the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston finds that rising domestic oil production has fundamentally changed how higher crude prices ripple through the U.S. economy. The result is a country that remains vulnerable to energy inflation but is far less likely to suffer the kind of employment damage that accompanied…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
top_signals.summary: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Public asked to help select UK wildlife to appear on new banknote series
top_signals.summary: The public are being asked to give their views on a selection of wildlife, native to the UK, that will appear on the next series of banknotes in a consultation launched today.

top_signals.title: Japan officials met metals and LNG firms in unusual Moscow Talks
top_signals.summary: The talks underscored the importance Tokyo and Moscow place on keeping channels of communication open.
